toolChanges.reason: Wrong stack - Python linter; discover found no owned Python files (all .py-adjacent tooling is absent). Also failed to install (python3-venv missing).
Wrong stack - Python security scanner; no owned Python in the repo.
Wrong stack - Python; no owned Python. Failed to install (venv).
Wrong stack - Java; zero owned Java files. Exited non-zero locally.
Wrong stack - Java/Jackson JSON; no owned Java.
Wrong stack - C/C++; the only C/C++ is vendored 3rd-party/open62541 interop examples, already excluded. Cannot auto-install locally.
Wrong stack - C/C++ security; same as cppcheck, no owned C/C++. Failed to install (venv).
Wrong stack - JS/TS; zero owned JS/TS files (all JS is vendored 3rd-party/node-opcua, excluded). Produced 0 issues.
Wrong stack - JS/TS; no owned JS/TS. Its only 2 findings were import/unambiguous misrouted onto .yml config files (false positives).

patternChanges.reason: Raised cyclomatic-complexity threshold 25->40 to match a mature OPC UA protocol library; keeps the genuinely complex functions flagged, drops the 25-39 band of routine protocol handlers.
Raised function-length threshold 100->200; 200+ line functions are the real outliers in this codebase (max ~347).
Raised file-size threshold 500->1000; flags only the genuinely giant hand-written files (up to 1910 lines) rather than every 500+ line module.
Raised parameter-count threshold 8->12; OPC UA service calls/structs legitimately take many params (max 14).

Lost-pattern recovery: this High/ErrorProne pattern found a real referenced-but-unassigned var in tools/check_version_numbers.sh in the baseline but was absent from the broad init --auto set. Restored.
Pure list-indent formatting nit (105 hits); subjective style, not a quality issue, on a Rust library's docs.
Inline-HTML nit (9 hits); intentional in these docs.

noisyButKept: Semgrep detect-insecure-websocket (7 hits in async-opcua-pubsub websocket transport) - legitimate ws:// transport code; kept per security guardrail for triage rather than disabled.
Semgrep gitleaks/detected-jwt-token (2 hits in async-opcua-core/src/logging/redact.rs) - intentional key/JWT redaction patterns in the secret-redaction module; kept for triage.
Trivy + Semgrep secret patterns - kept active; the sample x509 cert that produced the only false positives was file-excluded instead of disabling any pattern.

keyImprovements: Lizard complexity thresholds tuned to a mature OPC UA library (ccn 25->40, nloc 100->200, file-nloc 500->1000, params 8->12): 126 complexity warnings cut to 18 genuine outliers while keeping the truly complex/giant functions flagged.

Removed 9 wrong-stack tools (Python: PyLint/Bandit/Prospector; Java: PMD/jackson; C/C++: cppcheck/flawfinder; JS/TS: Biome/ESLint8) - zero owned files for any of them; this also eliminated ESLint's false-positive import/unambiguous hits on YAML config files.

markdownlint trimmed from 397 doc-formatting nits to 26 by disabling 12 pure-whitespace/style patterns and excluding non-source markdown (LICENSE, archived changelog, point-in-time audit/review reports).
Excluded Codacy's own .codacy/ working directory, which was being self-scanned (its result JSON embeds ws:// URLs and key/JWT fixtures), removing ~24 self-referential false positives.
Excluded the sample x509 demo cert (false-positive secret) and restored shellcheck_SC2154 (a real High finding dropped by init --auto). All genuine security coverage retained.
Net: 16->7 tools, 5204->873 patterns, 157->80 issues, analysis runtime 124s->57s, with every security concern still covered.
codacyYaml: .codacy.yaml updated with new exclusions: global .codacy/** and samples/simple-client/identity/sample-x509.pem; per-engine markdownlint excludes for LICENSE.md, docs/old_changelog.md, docs/*_AUDIT_*.md, docs/*_REVIEW_*.md. NOTE: Codacy Cloud reads .codacy.yaml from the DEFAULT branch only, so these file exclusions take effect on Cloud after .codacy.yaml is committed and pushed to master.

warnings: 2 cloud-only tools left unchanged: SonarC#, PSScriptAnalyzer (not reproducible by the local Analysis CLI).
9 wrong-stack tool disables blocked by the org 'Default coding standard'; user chose to leave the standard linked (those tools have no owned files, so they produce ~0 Cloud issues regardless).
Lizard threshold reconfiguration, markdownlint pattern disables, and the shellcheck_SC2154 restore all imported successfully (part of the 7 reconfigured tools).

Reanalysis reflects the imported PATTERN config only. The new .codacy.yaml file exclusions are not yet on master, so they were NOT applied in this reanalysis; committing .codacy.yaml to master will further reduce markdownlint findings on LICENSE/old_changelog/audit docs.

BestPractice +22 / Info +36 increase is a side effect of the init --auto reset: the kept tools (Agentlinter 102 patterns, markdownlint 31) carry broader pattern sets than the lean production config (Agentlinter 71, markdownlint 5). All on agent-instruction and doc files; trimmable on request.
